question: How should chaplaincy and grief be modeled?
short_answer: Chaplains should provide a structured grief and morale outlet after losses, because death in unit life needs ritual, counsel, and social processing.
evidence_summary: Army chaplains provided religious services, counseling, and moral support.
The Four Chaplains example shows sacrificial memory can become part of the unit story.
Loss is a morale event as much as a casualty event.

simulation_variables.effect: rises after deaths and suppresses performance until processed.
preserves named dead in the unit record.

game_implications: Funerals and memorials should be a real part of the campaign ledger.
The player should feel the cost of command socially, not only numerically.
